content:
- User asked whether all docs tasks are high priority.
- Read progress log first per repo instructions, then inspected `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and the active docs task files.
- Found current docs tasks under `story-build-docs-diataxis-from-zero` have no `<priority>` marker at all; they are just `not_started` with `<passes>false</passes>`.
- Cross-checked `.agents/skills/add-bug/SKILL.md`: `<priority>` is optional and should only be added if explicitly requested.
- Conclusion: docs tasks are not automatically high priority in this repo; only explicitly labeled tasks/bugs are high priority.

- User asked to make all docs tasks high priority and to report priorities of other tasks.
- Updated all seven files under `.ralph/tasks/story-build-docs-diataxis-from-zero/` to add `<priority>high</priority>` on the task header line.
- Verified all docs task headers now include the explicit high priority tag.
- Scanned the rest of `.ralph/tasks` currently present in this repo snapshot.
- Found two non-doc tasks under `story-managed-start-intent-architecture`; neither has any `<priority>` tag, so they are currently unprioritized.

- Read prior progress, scanned .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md, and inspected the active task files for blocker and priority tags.
- Found no active bug tasks and no blocked_by tags among active tasks; current_tasks_done is empty. The highest-precedence unblocked failing tasks are the high-priority docs tasks.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/story-build-docs-diataxis-from-zero/01-task-establish-diataxis-reread-and-draft-loop.md as next because it logically establishes the authoring loop required by the later docs tasks. Quit immediately after writing .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Read previous Ralph progress and `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-build-docs-diataxis-from-zero/01-task-establish-diataxis-reread-and-draft-loop.md`.
- Reading the task file next to determine whether I need to write the initial plan, review a prior plan, or execute an approved plan.

- Read the active task and the `create-docs` skill.
- Confirmed this is the initial planning phase: the task file does not yet contain `TO BE VERIFIED` or `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Inspected the docs tree enough to plan safely: `docs/src/` already has authored content, `docs/drafts/` does not exist yet.
- Next step is to patch the task file with a detailed implementation plan, end it with `TO BE VERIFIED`, and stop exactly there.

- Patched the active task file with a detailed implementation plan.
- Added the required `TO BE VERIFIED` marker at the end of the new plan section.
- Stopping now per task workflow: planning phase is complete and the next run should perform a skeptical plan review and alter at least one thing.

- Confirmed the active task file is in the `TO BE VERIFIED` phase and loaded the bundled `create-docs` skill because this task is defining the documentation workflow.
- Re-read the required Diataxis sources (`start-here`, `compass`, `how-to-use-diataxis`, and `reference`) to review the plan skeptically against the actual guidance.
- Found a concrete execution gap in the plan: `docs/drafts/` cannot survive as an empty directory in git, so the plan must require a tracked placeholder file inside `docs/drafts/` and verify that exact artifact.
- Next step is to patch the task file, alter the plan accordingly, replace `TO BE VERIFIED` with `NOW EXECUTE`, append progress again, and stop immediately.

- Completed the skeptical plan review for the active docs task and changed the implementation plan in-place.
- Altered the plan in a concrete way: execution must add a tracked placeholder file inside `docs/drafts/` because git will not preserve an empty directory.
- Replaced the end marker from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Stopping immediately per task workflow; the next run should execute the approved plan without further exploration.

- Reloaded the active task path and confirmed the current phase should be execution, not planning.
- The task is the docs workflow task, so I am reopening the task file and the create-docs skill only for execution context.
- Next I need to read the approved plan carefully, then apply exactly those changes, update docs if needed, and run the full required checks.

- Re-read the required Diataxis sources plus `docs/book.toml` and `docs/src/SUMMARY.md`.
- The guardrails are still exactly what the plan assumed: `docs/src/` has no imposed structure beyond `SUMMARY.md`, so execution should only add a tracked placeholder under `docs/drafts/` and then update the task record.
- Editing starts now, followed by a scope check before the full verification commands.

- Added `docs/drafts/.gitkeep` so the draft workspace is tracked without creating any docs structure under `docs/src/`.
- Updated the task description to say explicitly that only a tracked placeholder is allowed in `docs/drafts/` at this stage.
- Verifying the tree and task text now, then moving directly into the required `make` commands.

- Scope verification passed: only `docs/drafts/.gitkeep` was added under `docs/`, with no new workflow-only pages under `docs/src/`.
- `make docs-build`, `make check`, and `make test` have completed successfully.
- `make test-long` is currently still running; next is `make lint`, then task completion markers, task switch, commit, and push if everything stays green.

- Read the bundled Diataxis material from the create-docs skill plus the existing docs story tasks.
- Confirmed the story pattern: each docs task reuses exact Diataxis language, keeps the forms separate, and tells later runs to use ask-k2-docs only for prose.
- Next I need to insert a new docs task before the final verification task for contributor/codemap/codeguide material, keep the wording aligned to Diataxis source text, add the new writing rule about not using gate when test is meant, and renumber any later task references cleanly.

- User corrected the workflow: do not hand-edit .ralph/current_tasks.md; task insertion must go through the add-task-as-agent pattern and the summary is regenerated elsewhere.
- I am adjusting the approach accordingly: only story task files will change, with the final verification task moved to the next slot and a new contributor/codemap/codeguide task inserted before it.
- The new task needs to encode Diataxis wording from the bundled sources, require ask-k2-docs for prose work, and add the extra writing rule to avoid using gate where test is meant.

- Renamed the docs story final verification task from slot 07 to slot 08 so a new authoring task can exist before it.
- Added a new high-priority story task at `.ralph/tasks/story-build-docs-diataxis-from-zero/07-task-run-contributor-codemap-codeguide-pages-through-draft-check-edit-revise.md`.
- The new task mirrors the existing docs-task language and structure, cites the Diataxis contributor/complex-hierarchies wording, requires ask-k2-docs for prose-only help, and adds the explicit writing rule not to use gate when test is meant.

- The first `make test-long` run was invalid because I had launched it with a PTY; `docker compose exec` ended up in a stopped state and the gate timed out.
- Re-ran `make test-long` without a TTY, which is now progressing normally and already has ultra-long nextest passes.
- After that completes, I still need `make lint`, then I can update the task markers, switch task, commit, and push.

- All required gates are green now: `make docs-build`, `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, and `make lint`.
- Updated the task file to `status=done`, `passes=true`, checked the acceptance criteria, and added a short verification record.
- Running `.ralph/task_switch.sh` now, then I need to commit every changed file and push.
